what_it_does: This file is a contract-entry resource definition for the context-window; it specifies the per-session token budget identity, a hypothetical usage schema, the stateless state model, the in-process caller surfaces (/context, /compact, /clear, /btw), three planned operations (get, compact, clear) with bindings and verification notes, compaction/microcompaction semantics, errors, and links to session/transcript/cost-usage.
observations: All operations are marked planned because no Company fabric endpoint reads or drives the context window today.
Live usage is only available in-process via /context, the statusline context_window feed, and SDK compact_boundary messages.
The file explicitly refuses to claim a context:// address scheme because no accepting operation exists.
It includes a JSON schema for the usage shape a future fabric endpoint would serve.
Compaction is modeled as a boundary event, not a state machine; auto-compaction uses microcompaction first.
/clear starts a new session rather than compacting; /btw answers in an overlay without growing history.
Verification notes repeatedly state unverified and cite documentation rather than live endpoint tests.
The file references Atlas IDs CC-05.3, CC-05.4, CC-05.5 and adjacent resources like session, checkpoint, transcript, cost-usage.
standouts: The central fact: context-window has no fabric face; every op is planned/in-process only.
The field-reality honesty table distinguishing documented vs. verified behavior.
The refusal to mint a dead-end context:// scheme per §6 V7.
/btw as a side-channel that does not enter conversation history.
Compaction survival rules: root CLAUDE.md/auto-memory reload, subdirectory CLAUDE.md does not.
conventions: Uses contract-entry frontmatter with type, resource, summary, schemes, status, relates-to.
Uses contract:schema, contract:op, and contract:error fenced blocks.
Atlas references and status: planned for unimplemented operations.
Honest verification blocks with state: unverified and source citations.
Follows the no-dead-end-scheme rule by not claiming context://.
concerns: The contract repeatedly documents capabilities that do not exist, which could be misread as a spec for an imminent endpoint.
All verification is unverified, so the file is essentially documentation-driven rather than tested.
The /clear operation creates a new session, which may blur resource ownership between context-window and session.
The hypothetical schema is presented as a future endpoint shape but has no binding or version commitment.
notes: A self-aware contract entry that documents the context-window as an in-process-only resource today, with all fabric-facing operations honestly marked planned and unverified.
questions: What is the concrete timeline or condition for promoting context-window.get from planned to implemented?
How exactly is the statusline JSON feed consumed by external UI scripts?
Does /btw run on the same model instance or a separate lightweight context?
How does auto-compaction interact with manually invoked tools during a turn?
What distinguishes the planned context:// endpoint from the existing agent_sessions.turn usage block?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it presents itself as a contract entry for the context-window resource and does exactly that, while being unusually explicit that the fabric-facing parts are planned rather than live.
apparent_themes: Honest gap documentation for in-process-only capabilities.
Per-session resource scoping and session lifecycle coupling.
Token budget observability and compaction as a memory-management boundary.
Refusal to create addressing schemes without accepting operations.
Distinguishing live category breakdown from coarse per-turn usage proxies.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to hold a UI-contract shape for context-window management while preventing consumers from treating in-process slash commands and statusline feeds as stable, fabric-backed APIs.
novelty: The explicit field-reality honesty table and the legalistic refusal to register a context:// scheme because no operation accepts it are distinctive; most contract files would simply omit the scheme.
contribution: It contributes a precise, source-cited record of which context-window surfaces are in-process only, what a future fabric endpoint would need to expose, and the exact compaction/clear semantics, so a fused system can avoid exposing non-existent endpoints and can reuse these definitions when building one.
summary_for_embedding: Contract entry defining the per-session context-window resource, its planned get/compact/clear operations, in-process slash-command and statusline surfaces, compaction and microcompaction semantics, and the absence of a live Company fabric endpoint.
intention_for_embedding: To document the UI contract for observing and managing a session's live token budget while marking the fabric-facing read and drive operations as planned gaps.
suggested_fields: in_process_only to flag resources with no fabric endpoint.
adjacent_signals to list coarse proxies like agent_sessions.turn usage.
command_surface to capture slash-command bindings separately from SDK bindings.
proposed_edge_kinds: survives-compaction for memory files that reload.
resets-into for /clear creating a new session.
